tool_name: CNC Machining True Unit Cost (TCO) — Material, Tooling, Overhead & Scrap

inputs.name: Raw Material Billet / Blank Volume
inputs.hint: Billet volume before machining — NOT finished part volume

inputs.name: Material Density (ρ)
inputs.hint: Steel: 7850 | Aluminium: 2700 | Titanium: 4430 | Brass: 8500 | Cast iron: 7200
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 — Material density: CoV=0.01-0.03

inputs.name: Raw Material Unit Price
inputs.hint: Current market price — Al 6061: $2–4 | Steel 4140: $1–2 | Ti6Al4V: $25–45 | Inconel 718: $60–120
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Finished Part Net Volume
inputs.hint: Volume of finished part — used to calculate buy-to-fly ratio

inputs.name: Machine Hourly Rate (fully burdened)
inputs.hint: Depreciation + maintenance + energy + operator + floor space
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Total Cycle Time per Part
inputs.hint: From PRO_021 output or time study

inputs.name: Insert / Cutting Edge Cost
inputs.hint: Purchase price per single insert (not per cutting edge)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Number of Cutting Edges per Insert
inputs.hint: CNMG/SNMG=4 | WNMG=6 | Round insert=1 | Milling insert=2–4
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Tool Life per Cutting Edge (T)
inputs.hint: Measured or from Taylor equation — change at VB=0.3mm (ISO 3685)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

inputs.name: Coolant System Cost Rate
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Process Scrap / Rework Rate
inputs.hint: First-pass yield defect rate — target <1% for mature processes

inputs.name: Factory Overhead Absorption Rate
inputs.hint: Indirect costs absorbed to product: 20–60% typical job shop

formulas: Raw_Material_kg = raw_material_volume_m3 * material_density_kg_m3   // [kg] Raw billet weight
Material_Cost_USD = Raw_Material_kg * material_price_usd_kg   // [USD] Raw material cost per part
Buy_to_Fly_Ratio = raw_material_volume_m3 / finished_part_volume_m3   // [—] Billet-to-part volume ratio — waste indicator
Cost_per_Edge_USD = insert_cost_usd / cutting_edges_per_insert   // [USD] Cost per usable cutting edge
Tooling_Cost_per_Part_USD = (cycle_time_min / tool_life_per_edge_min) * Cost_per_Edge_USD   // [USD] Tooling consumed per part
Coolant_Cost_per_Part_USD = (cycle_time_min / 60) * coolant_cost_usd_hr   // [USD] Coolant cost per part
Direct_Cost_USD = Material_Cost_USD + Machining_Cost_USD + Tooling_Cost_per_Part_USD + Coolant_Cost_per_Part_USD   // [USD] Total direct cost
Pre_Scrap_Cost_USD = Direct_Cost_USD + Overhead_Cost_USD   // [USD] Cost before scrap penalty
Total_Unit_Cost_USD = Pre_Scrap_Cost_USD + Scrap_Penalty_USD   // [USD] True unit manufacturing cost
Annual_Cost_USD = annual_volume_parts > 0 ? Total_Unit_Cost_USD * annual_volume_parts : 0   // [USD/year] Annual cost

tool_life_minimum.error_msg: Tool life cannot be zero — division by zero in tooling cost calculation.

cutting_edges_minimum.error_msg: Cutting edges per insert must be at least 1.

finished_vs_raw_volume.error_msg: Finished part volume cannot be equal to or larger than raw billet volume. Material is removed during machining.

buy_to_fly_extreme.error_msg: Buy-to-fly ratio > 20: Over 95% of material becomes swarf. Verify volumes. For aerospace titanium forgings, ratios up to 10 are common — above 20 is exceptional.

smart_warnings.message: TOOLING COST EXCEEDS MACHINE TIME COST: Cutting speed is too high — tools are being consumed faster than economically optimal. Reduce Vc to extend tool life. Optimal Vc for minimum cost (Vcopt) is typically 20–30% below Vcmax.

High buy-to-fly ratio (>5): More than 80% of raw material becomes chip. Material waste is a dominant cost driver. Evaluate: (1) Near-net-shape forging or casting, (2) Additive manufacturing + hybrid machining, (3) Billet size optimisation.
Material cost >60% of total unit cost: Material is the dominant cost element. Price negotiation, alternative alloys, or supply chain changes will have greater impact than process optimisation.
Scrap rate > 5%: Significant quality cost. At this rate, scrap penalty is adding meaningful cost per part. Root cause analysis (8D / Ishikawa) required. Target: <1% for mature CNC processes.
Scrap rate 1–5%: Above world-class target. Each percentage point of scrap adds direct cost penalty. SPC implementation and tooling condition monitoring recommended.

Tool life < 15 minutes per edge: Very short tool life indicates aggressive cutting conditions or workpiece hardness issues. Economic tool life target is typically 20–45 min. Reduce Vc or check for built-up edge (BUE) conditions.

fmea.effect: Finished part volume cannot be equal to or larger than raw billet volume. Material is removed during machining.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Buy-to-fly ratio > 20: Over 95% of material becomes swarf. Verify volumes. For aerospace titanium forgings, ratios up to 10 are common — above 20 is exceptional.
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: TOOLING COST EXCEEDS MACHINE TIME COST: Cutting speed is too high — tools are being consumed faster than economically optimal. Reduce Vc to extend tool life. Optimal Vc for minimum cost (Vcopt) is typically 20–30% below Vcmax.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Tooling_Cost_per_Part_USD > Machining_Cost_USD

fmea.failureMode: High buy-to-fly ratio (>5): More than 80% of raw material becomes chip. Material waste is a dominant cost driver. Evaluate: (1) Near-net-shape forging or casting, (2) Additive manufacturing + hybrid machining, (3) Billet size optimisation.
Material cost >60% of total unit cost: Material is the dominant cost element. Price negotiation, alternative alloys, or supply chain changes will have greater impact than process optimisation.
Scrap rate > 5%: Significant quality cost. At this rate, scrap penalty is adding meaningful cost per part. Root cause analysis (8D / Ishikawa) required. Target: <1% for mature CNC processes.
Scrap rate 1–5%: Above world-class target. Each percentage point of scrap adds direct cost penalty. SPC implementation and tooling condition monitoring recommended.
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: scrap_rate_pct > 1 && scrap_rate_pct <= 5

fmea.failureMode: Tool life < 15 minutes per edge: Very short tool life indicates aggressive cutting conditions or workpiece hardness issues. Economic tool life target is typically 20–45 min. Reduce Vc or check for built-up edge (BUE) conditions.
